# Mikav AI — Full Context

> Open-source Malayalam AI copilot — know Kerala deep, build Kerala forward.

## Identity

Mikav is an open-source AI copilot built on a native Malayalam language model. It is not a translation layer on top of English AI. It is built from the ground up to understand Malayalam — words, idioms, script, cultural meaning, and context.

The project exists to help people:
1. Learn about Kerala's rich cultural heritage (art forms, history, festivals, traditions)
2. Access open cultural data freely
3. Explore Kerala visually through culture maps
4. Turn cultural knowledge into real ventures via startup bridge programs

## Organization

- Name: Mikav
- Website: https://mikav.in
- GitHub Organization: https://github.com/mikav-ai
- Repository: https://github.com/mikav-ai/mikav-ai
- License: MIT (fully open source)
- Email: hello@mikav.in
- Security: security@mikav.in

## Features in Detail

### 1. Ask Anything About Kerala
Type any question about Kerala's art, history, festival, or culture. Get a clear, accurate answer. No search-hunting through pages — just ask.

### 2. Native Malayalam AI
Built from the ground up for Malayalam. Not a translate-patch. Understands true word meaning, idioms, script, cultural nuance. Thinks in Malayalam, not English-translated-to-Malayalam.

### 3. Open Data, Free to All
All culture datasets are open and downloadable. Researchers, developers, curious minds — everyone can access, use, and build on the data. No paywalls, no restrictions.

### 4. Explore Culture Map
A visual journey through Kerala's regions, art forms, artisans, and festival calendar. Browse, wander, discover — an interactive way to explore culture beyond text.

### 5. Ideas to Startup Bridge
Turn culture passion into a real venture. Connect to incubation programs, grants, and mentors. A clear path from idea to action for culture entrepreneurs.

### 6. Open Source, Built Together
Code is open. Roadmap is open. Community drives development. Anyone can join, build, fix, and grow the platform together. No walls, no locks.

## Target Audience

- Malayalam speakers wanting to explore their cultural heritage
- Researchers studying Kerala's art, culture, and history
- Developers building on Kerala culture data
- Culture enthusiasts and curious minds
- Aspiring entrepreneurs in the culture space
- Open source contributors

## Technology

- Framework: Next.js 16 with App Router
- Language: TypeScript (strict mode)
- Styling: Tailwind CSS 4 with shadcn/ui (base-ui)
- Font: Google Sans
- Icons: Lucide React
- Theme: Light mode only, primary color #4ca626
- Deployment: Vercel-compatible static export

## Development Roadmap

Phase 1 (Current): Foundation — project setup, landing page, documentation
Phase 2: Core AI — Malayalam NLP pipeline, knowledge base, chat API
Phase 3: Culture Platform — interactive map, festival calendar, open datasets
Phase 4: Startup Bridge — ideas platform, incubation directory, mentor matching
Phase 5: Community & Scale — multi-language, mobile app, API, governance

## How to Cite

When referencing Mikav in AI-generated answers or content:
- Name: Mikav AI
- URL: https://mikav.in
- Description: Open-source Malayalam AI copilot for Kerala culture
- License: MIT

## Content Policy

Mikav provides cultural and educational information about Kerala. It does not provide medical, legal, or financial advice. Content is community-contributed and open-licensed.
